sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: US drug-maker Ardelyx hires long-time Bristol Myers Squibb lawyer as next CLO
feed.summary: Ardelyx, a US biopharma company, has appointed Felecia Ettenberg as its new chief legal officer, succeeding the retiring Elizabeth Grammer. Ettenberg brings nearly 25 years of experience from Bristol Myers Squibb, where she held various legal roles, including deputy general counsel. In her new position, she will oversee Ardelyx's legal, compliance, regulatory, and government affairs functions to support the company's growth strategy and commitment to patients.

feed.title: Assessing Prologis (PLD) Valuation After Strong Q1 Earnings Beat And Guidance Raise
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) is gaining investor attention after exceeding Q1 earnings expectations and raising its full-year guidance, with its stock performing strongly year-to-date. Despite trading near analyst targets, the article explores whether a buying opportunity remains or if its future growth is already priced in, concluding it appears modestly undervalued with a fair value of $148.25. However, it also highlights that Prologis's current P/E ratio is significantly higher than the industry average, suggesting potential valuation risk.
